2604.19139.title: The Rise of Verbal Tics in Large Language Models: A Systematic Analysis Across Frontier Models
2604.19139.paper_summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) continue to evolve through alignment techniques such as Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and Constitutional AI, a growing and increasingly conspicuous phenomenon has emerged: the proliferation of verbal tics -- repetitive, formulaic linguistic patterns that pervade model outputs. These range from sycophantic openers ("That's a great question!", "Awesome!") to pseudo-empathetic affirmations ("I completely understand your concern", "I'm right here to catch you") and overused vocabulary ("delve", "tapestry", "nuanced"). In this paper, we present a systematic analysis of the verbal tic phenomenon across eight state-of-the-art LLMs: GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Grok 4.2, Doubao-Seed-2.0-pro, Kimi K2.5, DeepSeek V3.2, and MiMo-V2-Pro. Utilizing a custom evaluation framework for standardized API-based evaluation, we assess 10,000 prompts across 10 task categories in both English and Chinese, yielding 160,000 model responses. We introduce the Verbal Tic Index (VTI), a composite metric quantifying tic prevalence, and analyze its correlation with sycophancy, lexical diversity, and human-perceived naturalness. Our findings reveal significant inter-model variation: Gemini 3.1 Pro exhibits the highest VTI (0.590), while DeepSeek V3.2 achieves the lowest (0.295). We further demonstrate that verbal tics accumulate over multi-turn conversations, are amplified in subjective tasks, and show distinct cross-lingual patterns. Human evaluation (N = 120) confirms a strong inverse relationship between sycophancy and perceived naturalness (r = -0.87, p < 0.001). These results underscore the "alignment tax" of current training paradigms and highlight the urgent need for more authentic human-AI interaction frameworks.
2604.19139.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）通過如人類反饋強化學習（RLHF）和憲法人工智慧等對齊技術不斷演進，一個日益明顯的現象出現了：語言習慣的激增——重複的、公式化的語言模式充斥著模型的輸出。這些模式從諂媚的開場白（「這是一個很好的問題！」、「太棒了！」）到偽同理的肯定（「我完全理解你的擔憂」、「我就在這裡支持你」）以及過度使用的詞彙（「深入探討」、「掛毯」、「細膩」）不等。在本文中，我們對八個最先進的LLMs進行了語言習慣現象的系統分析：GPT-5.4、Claude Opus 4.7、Gemini 3.1 Pro、Grok 4.2、Doubao-Seed-2.0-pro、Kimi K2.5、DeepSeek V3.2和MiMo-V2-Pro。我們利用自定義評估框架進行標準化的基於API的評估，評估了10,000個提示，涵蓋10個任務類別，並在英語和中文中產生了160,000個模型回應。我們引入了語言習慣指數（VTI），這是一個量化習慣流行度的綜合指標，並分析其與諂媚、詞彙多樣性和人類感知自然性之間的相關性。我們的研究結果顯示出顯著的模型間變異性：Gemini 3.1 Pro的VTI最高（0.590），而DeepSeek V3.2的VTI最低（0.295）。我們進一步展示了語言習慣在多輪對話中累積，在主觀任務中被放大，並顯示出明顯的跨語言模式。人類評估（N = 120）確認了諂媚與感知自然性之間的強負相關（r = -0.87，p < 0.001）。這些結果強調了當前訓練範式的「對齊稅」，並突顯了對更真實的人類-人工智慧互動框架的迫切需求。
2604.19139.author: Shuai Wu et.al.
2604.19139.authors: Shuai Wu, Xue Li, Yanna Feng, Yufang Li, Zhijun Wang, Ran Wang
